trialDescriptions.title: Effektivität und Sicherheit von Bronchialer Thermoplastie unter Einbeziehung des Mittellappens
trialDescriptions.summary:
Bronchiale Thermoplastie (BT) ist eine zugelassene Therapieform bei schwerem, therapieresistentem Asthma bronchiale. Das gesamte Bronchialsystem wird endoskopisch mittels Radiofrequenzablation behandelt, der Mittellappen ausgelassen. 
Bei 15 Patienten wurde nach expliziter Aufklärung die BT auch im Mittellappen durchgeführt. Die jetzige retrospektive Analyse evaluiert Sicherheit, Lebensqualität, Funktionstest vor und 90 Tage nach BT.
trialDescriptions.scientificSummary: Wissenschaftliche Kurzbeschreibung in englischer Sprache verfügbar

trialDescriptions.title: Efficiacy and safety of Bronchial Thermoplasty, when applied also in the Middle Lobe Bronchus
trialDescriptions.summary:
Bronchial Thermoplasty is indicated in patient suffering from severe and symptomatic bronchial asthma despite maximal medical therapy. However, treatment of the middle lobe bronchus is currently not recommended. To investigate the safety and efficacy of Bronchial Thermoplasty including the middle lobe bronchus. 
Methods
BT was performed in 15 consecutive patients, quality of life and pulmonary function was characterized before and 90 days after BT completion. Furthermore we established a clean-up bronchosocopy following every BT application.
trialDescriptions.scientificSummary:
Study subjects and design
All patients referred to our centers (Ruhrlandklinik Essen and Martha-Maria Halle-Dölau) between August 2013 and January 2016 were consecutively screened regarding their eligibility for BT. Both centers have a dedicated program for patients with severe asthma. Adult patients with an established diagnosis of severe, poorly controlled asthma under adequate maximum maintenance medication including high dose inhaled corticosteroid (ICS) and long-acting betaagonist (LABA) and also, if necessary, additive tiotropium as long acting muscarin-antagonists (LAMA) and OCS were included in our study if they were stable and free of exacerbation four weeks before BT.  Controller medication was not changed within 4 weeks before BT. In contrast to former randomized-control studies (RCT) permanent decline of FEV1 was no exclusion criteria, whenever COPD could be definitely excluded. This was assumed if there was no relevant smoking history (<10 package years), no radiographic signs of emphysema and a documented positive reaction to bronchospasmolytic agents in the past. Baseline investigation included total Serum-IgE, absolute number of blood eosinophils, pulmonary function testing (PFT) with bodyplethysmography and QoL-evaluation with Asthma Questionnaire of Life Quality (AQLQ). A screening bronchoscopy was only performed if additional factors of asthma persistence had to be excluded. Patients older than 40 years or those with reported mucus expectoration received Chest-CT-scans to rule out any structural alterations. Three months follow up after BT completion included AQLQ, PFT and evaluation of OCS necessity. 

Methods
BT was performed as described earlier [6]. Briefly: BT treatment consists of three bronchoscopies, performed in total intravenous anesthesia (TIVA), either with rigid bronchoscopy or laryngeal mask. Periods between the treatments ranged between 4-8 weeks. A middle lobe treatment was included, if the proximal middle lobe bronchus was easy to intubate with a bronchoscope with an outer diameter of at least 6.2 mm. Treatment of the middle lobe bronchus was performed together with the right lower lobe in the first session, the second treatment involved the left lower lobe and the last treatment both upper lobes. Subsequently a bronchoscope with a maximum outer diameter of 4.2 mm and a working channel of 2.0 mm was used. After advancing the bronchoscope in the airways as peripheral as possible the ALAIR®-catheter was pushed forward into the particular subsegment until the last proximal mark on the catheter was visible and no distal resistance was noted. All segments with a diameter of 3-10 mm were treated and the number of activations was counted. When applying BT in the medial segment of the middle lobe bronchus (RB 5), cardiac arrhythmic events other than the intrinsic ECG-pattern were observed. After middle lobe bronchus treatment and in case of respiratory deterioration a chest-x-ray was performed 48 hours (h) after treatment. A clean-up bronchoscopy was offered to all patients and upon agreement it was performed 48-72 h after BT, even in case of a stable respiratory situation. 
All patients were hospitalized for at least three days and received OCS (50 mg daily) two days prior until two days after the procedure. Adverse events were recorded until six weeks after BT and defined as treatment-related. Informed consent was obtained from every patient. The study was approved by the local ethics committee of University of Duisburg-Essen (approval number. 6767-BO).

Analysis
Student’s t test for paired samples was used to examine mean differences in changes from baseline to follow-up values. An increase of 0.5 points in AQLQ was considered necessary for minimal clinically important difference (MCID) [7]. A p-value of less than 0.05 was considered statistically significant. Graphical analysis was performed with Microsoft Excel-Software (Professional Version 2010).

observationalGroups.comment: Patienten mit schwerem, trotz optimaler medikamentöser Therapie unkontrolliertem Asthma bronchiale werden mittels Bronchialer Thermoplastie behandelt, dies ist eine bronchoskopische Radiofrequenzablation. Der bisherige Behandlungsalgorithmus umfasste den gesamten Bronchialbaum, aber ohne Mittellappen. Wir führten diese Behandlung nun auch additiv im Mittellappen durch und analysieren hinsichtlich Sicherheit und Effektivität. Die Analyse erfolgt retrospektiv.

Patients with severe and poorly controlled bronchial Asthma receive bronchoscopic radio frequency ablation (RFA), called Bronchial Thermoplasty. Historically the Treatment protocol leaves out the middle lobe Bronchus.
We include the middle lobe Bronchus and Analyse retrospectively efficiacy and safety.

characteristicDescriptions.primaryOutcome: Sicherheit 90 Tage nach Bronchialer Thermoplastie mit dem Mittellappen
characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome: Lungenfunktion (Bodyplethysmographie), Lebensqualität (AQLQ-Asthma Quality of Life Questionnaire), Orale Steroid-Dosierung, Asthma Exazerbationen vor und nach Bronchialer Thermoplastie, Behandlungskomplikationen,

characteristicDescriptions.primaryOutcome: Safety of BT including the middle lobe bronchus
characteristicDescriptions.secondaryOutcome: Pulmonary function (bodyplethysmography), Quality of Life (AQLQ-Asthma Quality of Life Questionnaire), dosage of orale corticosteroids, treatment complications, asthma exacerbations before and after Bronchial Thermoplasty

publications.description: Bronchial Thermoplasty Including the Middle Lobe Bronchus Significantly Improves Lung Function and Quality of Life in Patients Suffering from Severe Asthma

recruitmentDescriptions.exclusionCriteria: Contraindications of bronchoscopy and radiofrequency ablation
